report_name: Ransomware Roundup: LockBit, Akira, and Others Claim Victims Across Multiple Sectors

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that targeted and claimed responsibility for an attack on Advanced Business Systems, a US-based IT provider, on July 1, 2026.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: LockBit
threat_actors.description: A notorious ransomware gang that claimed attacks on two German companies, ComTRI and Gies Dienstleistungen, on July 1, 2026.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: TheGentlemen
threat_actors.description: A prolific ransomware group that listed four victims on its data leak site on July 1, 2026, including a US local government and companies in Taiwan, Thailand, and Japan.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: BlackNevas
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that targeted the Arkın Group conglomerate, listing it as a victim on July 1, 2026.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: SETTRA
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that attacked City Lumber Company, a US building materials supplier, and listed it as a victim on July 1, 2026.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: Genesis
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that listed the non-profit law firm Brooklyn Defender Services as a victim on July 1, 2026.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups deployed their payloads to encrypt victim networks as the final stage of their intrusions, impacting multiple sectors.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: A recommended hunting hint for ransomware attacks is to monitor for the use of vssadmin to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique to prevent system recovery.

malware.name: Akira Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload associated with the Akira threat actor group, used in attacks culminating on July 1, 2026.

malware.name: LockBit Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload associated with the LockBit threat actor group, used in attacks against two German companies.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: The vssadmin tool was mentioned as a utility to monitor for hunting ransomware precursors, specifically its use in deleting volume shadow copies.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: PsExec was mentioned as a common hacking tool whose presence or execution is a high-confidence indicator of an active intrusion related to ransomware attacks.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Mimikatz was mentioned as a common hacking tool for credential dumping, and its presence is a high-confidence indicator of an active intrusion.

identities.name: Advanced Business Systems
identities.description: A US-based IT and office solutions provider targeted by the Akira ransomware group.

identities.name: ComTRI
identities.description: A German IT systems firm claimed as a victim by the LockBit ransomware group.

identities.name: Gies Dienstleistungen
identities.description: A German facility management company claimed as a victim by the LockBit ransomware group.

identities.name: Boyne City
identities.description: A local government in Michigan, USA, listed as a victim by TheGentlemen ransomware group.

identities.name: Climax Technology
identities.description: A Taiwanese technology manufacturer listed as a victim by TheGentlemen ransomware group.

identities.name: Comp Trading Co., Ltd.
identities.description: An IT company in Thailand listed as a victim by TheGentlemen ransomware group.

identities.name: DHC Corporation
identities.description: A major Japanese manufacturer listed as a victim by TheGentlemen ransomware group.

identities.name: Arkın Group
identities.description: A diversified conglomerate targeted by the BlackNevas ransomware group.

identities.name: City Lumber Company
identities.description: A US building materials supplier attacked by the SETTRA ransomware group.

identities.name: Brooklyn Defender Services
identities.description: A non-profit law firm listed as a victim by the Genesis ransomware group.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1b9c-0e4d-4f2a-9b8c-7d6e5f4a3b2c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware precursor technique mentioned in the article's hunting hints.

indicators.name: PsExec Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PsExec Execution
id: 9d8c7b6a-5f4e-4d3c-2b1a-0987654321fe
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a common tool used by attackers for lateral movement, as suggested in the article's hunting hints.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
tags:
  - attack.t1569.002
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    OriginalFileName: 'psexec.exe'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith: '\PSTools\PsExec.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a tool mentioned in the article as a high-confidence indicator of an active intrusion.

indicators.name: Mimikatz Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Mimikatz Execution
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Mimikatz, a common tool for credential dumping, as suggested in the article's hunting hints.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.credential_access
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  keywords:
    - 'mimikatz'
    - 'lsadump::'
    - 'sekurlsa::'
  condition: 1 of keywords
indicators.description: Detects the execution of Mimikatz, a tool mentioned in the article as a high-confidence indicator of an active intrusion.
